items.title: 🔪 Knives — plant your own plot twists
items.body: Attach story beats to any character in the Character Workshop (new Knives tab): secrets, debts, rivals waiting in the wings. When the Doom Counter strikes, one present character with armed knives is chosen at random and THEIR knives become the twist options — you won't know whose past surfaces until it does. Used knives can be re-armed. Turn it on per story: Doom Counter → Enable Knives (this chat).

items.title: Generate Knives with themes
items.body: Out of ideas? The Workshop's Generate Knives button asks your AI for 5 suggestions grounded in the character and your current chat. Pick a theme first — Mixed, Betrayal, Enemies, Debts, Old Flames, Secrets, Regrets, or Fortune — then tick the ones worth keeping. Regrets and Fortune produce sympathetic and lucky beats, not just villainy.

items.title: Trap Mode draws knives too
items.body: With Trap Mode on, a random armed knife from a random present character is injected silently. Your own pre-planted surprises can now ambush you.

items.title: Custom Scene Tracker fields
items.body: Add your own fields to the Scene Tracker (Tracker Editor → Info Box → Custom Scene Fields): a name, an emoji icon, and an instruction telling the AI what to track. They render in every layout — grid, banner, HUD, and ticker — support inline editing, and can be included in History Persistence.

items.title: Character Aliases — no more duplicate cards
items.body: The AI upgraded 'Sarah' to 'Sarah Greenfield' and DES spawned a second card? Add the full name as an Alias on Sarah's card (Workshop → Identity). Any tracker data using an alias now resolves to the existing card — portrait, color, sheet, and knives included — while the AI stays free to use the name in prose.

items.title: Doom Counter picker: Reroll and Cancel
items.body: Don't like the twist options? Reroll for a fresh set. Not in the mood at all? Cancel dismisses the picker and resets the counter.

items.title: Knife prompts are editable
items.body: The knife injection template and the Generate Knives rules join the other prompts in Advanced → Edit AI Prompts, each with a Restore Default button.

items.title: Name Ban removed
items.body: The old (hidden, unfinished) Name Ban system is gone — Character Aliases replaces its useful half without nagging your model. Elara, Seraphina, Nyx, and Voss remain at large; the knife generator at least refuses to hire them.
